NVDA.reasoning: No valid breakout setup is present. The relevant range boundaries are: upper consolidation resistance at $208.20 (former 15-test support, now resistance per wiki; confirmed breach on April 30 with 1.52x avg volume = bearish breakout already completed) and base support at $197.12 (recent low). The downside breakout from the $208.20 level has already fired and partially exhausted — price dropped from $216.83 to $198.45 (-8.5%) in 4 sessions. The breakout is done; it is not my setup anymore. For a new bullish breakout opportunity, I require a confirmed daily close above $203.00 (hourly pivot, 12-test volume-confirmed resistance) with volume 1.5x+ the 20-day average (current: 0.87x — no confirmation). Bollinger Band width on the daily is 0.2224 (not compressed; volatility already expanded). Hourly BB width is 0.1123, tighter but with price near the lower band (pct_b 0.17) and hourly RSI-21 at 18.82 — deeply oversold but no bullish reversal candle yet. Squeeze status: no active squeeze; the hourly expansion already fired bearishly (MACD histogram -1.82, -DI 36.05 >> +DI 12.97). Wiki confirms both stops were hit at $205.30 on April 30; prior lesson flags a false breakout pattern on NVDA (EMA pullback failed, -$63.20). The $208.20 level is now overhead resistance. Price may be forming a base at $197-$199, consistent with the wiki's target entry zone ($197.50-$199.50), but a base is not a breakout — I need to see the coil complete and a confirmed volume surge above resistance before acting. No entry until close above $203.00 on 1.5x+ volume.

Vote count: 3 bullish (swing_mean_reversion 42, swing_catalyst_news 62, swing_macro_context 55), 2 neutral (swing_trend_momentum 35, swing_breakout 38), 0 bearish. Weighted toward bullish but all three bullish agents are explicitly CONDITIONAL — they require a confirmed hourly reversal candle at the Fib 38.2% / EMA-21 confluence ($196.75–$199.50) AND the hourly MACD histogram turning positive before entry is valid. Neither condition is currently met. The setup structure is correct: price pulled back -8.5% from $216.83 high to $198.45, daily trend intact (ADX 56.9, full EMA uptrend alignment), AMD earnings catalyst imminent (AI capex read-through), and macro is risk-on with S&P near ATHs. However, the hourly layer is actively bearish (-DI 36.05 vs +DI 12.97, MACD histogram -1.82) with OBV showing distribution on both timeframes. WIKI MEMORY DIAL-DOWN: The only closed NVDA trade in 30 days is the April 30 EMA pullback Fib dip-buy, which was stopped at $205.30 with -$63.20 P&L. This exact same setup type now scores a 0% win rate. The wiki explicitly flagged $196.75–$197.67 as the true entry zone and required bullish confirmation candle before entry — the prior stop-out occurred because entry was taken WITHOUT confirmation. A conditional buy at the confluence is appropriate; a blind entry at current price ($198.45) is not. No open NVDA position exists so there is no stacking concern. The broader book is balanced (3 long / 3 short). AMD catalyst is binary — a negative AI capex report from AMD would invalidate the thesis immediately and is an unpriced tail risk within the 1-3 day window. Confidence is capped at 44 (below the typical 50 threshold for action) because: (1) the required confirmation candle is absent, (2) this setup type is 0-for-1 in the last 30 days on this exact ticker, (3) the AMD read-through is unresolved. Consensus is bullish-conditional — do not act until trigger conditions are met.
NVDA.key_conflicts: The primary conflict is between swing_trend_momentum (neutral, 35) and the three bullish agents. Trend-momentum is explicitly waiting for ROC-5d to turn positive and the daily MACD histogram to cross above zero — it interprets the current short-term momentum contraction (-4.72% ROC-5d, slightly negative MACD histogram) as unfinished selling, not a buy signal. Swing_breakout (neutral, 38) sees the same data differently: the bearish breakout from $208.20 has already fired and exhausted, but a new bullish setup requires a confirmed daily close above $203.00 on 1.5x volume — a higher bar than the other agents require. This disagreement is telling us something important: the DAILY trend structure is fully intact (all EMAs bullish, ADX very strong) but the SHORT-TERM price action is still distributing. The bullish camp (catalyst-news and macro-context) is willing to buy the dip into the confluence zone with a tight stop because the catalyst window and macro backdrop justify the risk. The neutral camp argues that without momentum re-acceleration, buying is premature. This is NOT a coin flip — it is a legitimate 'trend intact but near-term momentum stalled' setup where the entry trigger matters more than the directional call. Additionally, all agents have absorbed the wiki's April 30 lesson: the prior NVDA stop-out on this exact setup type at this exact zone is a known risk. This lesson appears to have REDUCED all confidence scores across the board compared to what they would otherwise be.

Fed policy transition (Powell -> Warsh, May 15) introduces macro uncertainty near the trade window

NVDA.reasoning: TREND ASSESSMENT: Daily trend structure is intact and strong — full EMA uptrend alignment (EMA-10 202.99 > EMA-21 197.74 > EMA-50 190.83), ADX-14 at 56.9 (very strong trend), and price is sitting right at the EMA-21 / 38.2% Fib confluence ($196.75–$197.74), which is precisely the pullback entry zone this strategy targets. The macro regime is risk-on (S&P 500 near ATHs, April was best month since 2020). MOMENTUM ASSESSMENT: Short-term momentum is NOT accelerating — ROC-5d is -4.72 and ROC-10d is -1.60 (both negative), indicating the recent sell-off from $216.83 high has not yet exhausted itself. MACD histogram is slightly negative (-0.11) with no bullish crossover. Strategy rule: 'wait for re-acceleration when ROC is contracting and MACD histogram is shrinking.' HOURLY DIVERGENCE: The hourly frame is actively bearish (-DI 36.05 > +DI 12.97, hourly MACD histogram at -1.82, hourly RSI-21 at 18.82 — severely oversold but no reversal signal yet). Hourly ROC is negative across all windows. No hourly/daily alignment = no confirmation. WIKI ALIGNMENT: Prior entry (wiki 2026-05-01) flagged exactly this $196.75–$197.67 zone as the re-entry trigger, BUT required a confirmed bullish reversal candle before entry. No such candle is present at current reading. LESSON APPLIED: The prior EMA pullback Fib dip-buy on NVDA resulted in -$63.20 loss when no bullish confirmation appeared before stop hit (April 30). Lowering confidence to 35 — the setup is structurally forming but momentum has not yet re-accelerated. VERDICT: Neutral. Watch for: hourly RSI-21 bouncing from oversold + daily ROC-5d turning positive + MACD histogram crossing back above zero on daily as re-entry triggers.

1. MACRO REGIME: Cautiously risk-on. S&P 500 at all-time highs (7,200+), April 2026 best month since 2020. Fed held at 3.5%-3.75% with 4 dissents (most since Oct 1992) — internal disagreement signals policy uncertainty ahead. Powell transition on May 15 to likely successor Kevin Warsh introduces additional uncertainty. Iran peace talks removed the dominant left-tail oil-spike risk (WTI -2% May 1). Inflation remains elevated but the worst-case energy scenario has eased. Net regime read: risk-on with near-term policy transition overhang. AI capex supercycle narrative intact (Alphabet, Amazon beat, Azure +40%), but MSFT post-earnings selloff and Meta -9% capex shock introduced near-term AI-spend uncertainty — directly relevant to NVDA demand thesis. 2. RELATIVE STRENGTH: NVDA is SHORT-TERM underperforming. 5d ROC -4.72%, 10d ROC -1.6% vs broad market risk-on. However, 21d ROC +12.92% shows NVDA led the prior sector upswing. Price broke below EMA-5 and EMA-10, now testing EMA-20/21 confluence — structure is intact at all longer MAs (above EMA-20/50/200) but the recent pullback puts it below short-term averages. Hourly: price is below all hourly EMAs (24, 48, 120) with hourly ADX -DI (36.05) > +DI (12.97) showing active intraday downward momentum. OBV trending down on both daily and hourly — distribution signal, not accumulation. 3. ASYMMETRIC R/R: Entry ~$198.45 at EMA-21 / Fib 38.2% confluence zone ($196.75-$197.67 per thesis). Nearest resistance: $203 (hourly pivot, volume-confirmed, 19 tests at $198.70). Target: $209-210 (prior support zone turned resistance, below recent highs). Stop: $193.50 (below Fib 38.2% support at $196.75 plus buffer; a close below the confluence zone invalidates the thesis). Upside: $209 - $198.45 = $10.55 (~5.3%). Downside: $198.45 - $193.50 = $4.95 (~2.5%). Asymmetry: ~2.1:1 — meets minimum 2:1 bar, but barely. 4. CAPITAL PRESERVATION FLAGS: NVDA fundamentals remain exceptional (61% revenue growth, 144% EPS growth, 59% operating margin, D/E 0.11 near-zero leverage). No balance sheet concerns. ATR 3.23% daily is elevated but consistent with NVDA's norm and the current macro regime. KEY LESSON FROM PRIOR RUN (Apr 30): The last EMA pullback Fib dip-buy at this zone was stopped out at $205.30 (entered too high). The wiki thesis now explicitly identifies $196.75-$197.67 as the real entry zone — current price at $198.45 is AT the identified confluence. However, the prior lesson also warns 'do not enter without bullish confirmation candle.' Hourly RSI-21 at 18.82 is deeply oversold — a potential early sector rotation signal per the prompt — but no bullish hourly candle confirmation is evident yet. OVERALL: Risk-on macro and intact AI thesis support a bullish lean. Price is at the thesis-defined support confluence. Hourly oversold condition suggests bounce potential. However, OBV distribution on both timeframes, ongoing short-term momentum weakness, and the 0% win rate on this exact setup type (EMA pullback Fib dip-buy) warrant reduced confidence. Enter only on a confirmed bullish candle at the confluence, not blindly at current price.

Branch B (buy-the-dip) is near-firing but UNCONFIRMED — entry not yet valid. Branch A (fade-the-extreme) does NOT fire: daily Z-score 0.94 is within normal range, RSI-14 58.08 is neutral, Bollinger pct_b 0.528 sits near the middle band — no statistical extreme present.

Branch B setup: NVDA is in a strong uptrend (ADX 56.9, +DI 29.18 >> -DI 15.93, full EMA stack bullish). Price has pulled back -8.5% from the $216.83 swing high and is now testing the 38.2% Fibonacci retracement at $196.75 — price $198.45 is only 0.87% above this level. Confluence confirmed: hourly pivot low $198.70 (19 tests, volume-confirmed) and daily pivot high $197.62 (6 tests) both cluster at the 38.2% Fib zone. Pullback volume is DECLINING (relative volume 0.87, volume_bias_10d: down) — a healthy characteristic.

Hourly layer strongly supports a bounce: RSI-21 = 18.82 (deeply oversold, below 25 threshold), Bollinger pct_b = 0.166 (near lower band). However, the hourly MACD histogram is still -1.8166 (no positive turn yet) and price remains below all hourly MAs (EMA-24, SMA-24, EMA-48, SMA-48) with bearish ADX internals (-DI 36.05 >> +DI 12.97). No bullish divergence detected on either timeframe. The most recent daily candle (May 1) closed near the low ($198.45 close vs $203.00 high) with no hammer or engulfing reversal pattern confirmed.

Wiki lesson penalty: The prior identical 'EMA pullback Fib dip-buy' setup on NVDA failed (0% win rate, 1 trade, -$63.20). The wiki pre-identified this exact $197.50–$199.50 entry zone but explicitly required a confirmed hourly reversal candle AND MACD histogram turning positive before entry — neither condition is met yet. The $208.20 structural support level was also breached on 1.52x volume, weakening the trend structure. Confidence is reduced accordingly from what would otherwise be ~60 to 42.

Conditional bullish: the setup will be valid once a reversal candle (hammer, bullish engulfing) prints at the 38.2% Fib zone AND hourly MACD histogram turns positive. Entry zone: $196.75–$199.50.

CATALYST WINDOW: AMD earnings imminent (1-3 trading days, AI data center read-through) + NVDA Q1 FY2027 earnings ~May 20, 2026 (~12 trading days). Hard exit deadline ~May 17. NET NEWS SENTIMENT: Moderately bullish. Positive drivers: 94% analyst Buy, avg PT $270 (~35% upside from $199.57), Blackwell Ultra shipping with 2-gen competitive lead vs AMD/Intel, Alphabet +7% and Amazon +2% post-earnings confirming AI GPU capex, Apple Q2 beat adds positive Magnificent Seven sentiment, NVDA forward P/E 18.97 with $96.68B FCF. Negative drivers: Meta fell 9% on AI capex shock ($125-145B raised guidance) creating near-term sector ROI concern; H20 China export ban ($4.5B charge) fully priced but structural drag; custom silicon (Google TPUs, AWS Trainium) cited as long-term threat. INSIDER ACTIVITY: Quiet in current 30-day window (0 trades). Background historically bearish (706/735 historic sales) but prior CFO Kress sales were at $171-177 — well below current $198.45 — scheduled 10b5-1 sales, not an acute signal. TECHNICAL SETUP — CONSTRUCTIVE BASE: Price ($198.45) resting at the confluence of daily 20/21 EMA/SMA support zone ($197-198), just above daily Fib 38.2% retracement at $196.75 from the $164.27-$216.83 swing. Daily RSI-14 at 58.08 (not overbought), ADX 56.9 (strong trend), price above all key MAs (20/21/50/200 on daily). Hourly RSI at 18.82 (deeply oversold — bounce due). Hourly volume confirmed support at $198.70 (19 tests). RISK FACTORS: Prior NVDA stop-out on 2026-04-30 at $205.30 on similar pre-earnings dip-buy setup (EMA pullback Fib dip-buy, -$63.20); however AMD catalyst timing is new trigger not present then. Meta capex shock overhang persists. Hourly trend still bearish (minus_di 36.05 > plus_di 12.97). AMD read-through is binary — negative AI capex report would invalidate thesis quickly. Confidence: 62 (bullish with catalyst tailwinds but recent stop-out pattern acknowledged).

reasoning.sizing_model: PM picks conviction + account_risk_pct (≤2.5%); qty = (capital × risk_pct) / |entry - stop|. Hard cap: position ≤ 30% of capital ($643). Vol/correlation are context for PM to consider qualitatively.
